defaults.title: Agent
defaults._comment: Global defaults for every ping. Resolution order for each field: --flag > channel config > this defaults block > built-in. Override any of these on a single channel below, or per call with a flag.

policy._comment: notify-only delivery policy. min_urgency: only fire notify at/above this level (low|normal|high) -- lower ones return 'suppressed' (exit 0). min_interval: drop a notify within N seconds of the last delivered one -- returns 'throttled' (exit 0); 0 = off. Blocking verbs (ask/confirm/choose) are never gated, since the agent is waiting.

system._comment: macOS notification sound. 'auto' gives each verb its own tone (notify=Glass, ask=Ping, choose=Pop, confirm=Sosumi) so you know by ear what the agent needs. Or set one name (Glass, Hero, Ping, Sosumi, Submarine, ...), a per-verb map like {"confirm": "Sosumi"}, or "none" to silence. Custom: drop a .aiff in ~/Library/Sounds and use its name. Per-call override: --sound NAME.

banner._comment: Optional (macOS). Run: agentping setup-logo. Routes plain notify to a logo banner; ask/confirm/choose stay on system dialogs. Requires: brew install terminal-notifier

phone._comment: Subscribe to this topic in the ntfy app. You get a push; reply to the topic and the agent reads it back. Use --channel phone, or set this as default_channel on headless boxes.

iphone._comment: macOS only, uses YOUR Messages account (no API key, no fee). 'to' is a phone number or Apple ID; set service to 'SMS' to relay through a connected iPhone (Text Message Forwarding). Two-way: send a question and the agent reads your reply back from the Messages database. One-time setup: sign in to Messages; allow your terminal/agent to control Messages (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation) to send; grant it Full Disk Access to read replies. With Full Disk Access it also confirms delivery and reports a 'Not Delivered' as an error; add "verify": false to skip that check, "verify_timeout": 6 to bound the wait.

sms._comment: Outbound SMS via Twilio (the external-provider path; reaches non-Apple phones and works from a non-Mac host). notify only. Leave account_sid/auth_token blank here and set $TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID / $TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN in the environment to keep secrets out of the file. 'from' is your Twilio number, 'to' is the recipient.

slack._comment: One-way (notify only). Slack incoming webhooks want a 'text' field, which is exactly the default body, so no 'body' template is needed.

discord._comment: One-way (notify only). Discord webhooks want a 'content' field, not 'text', so this body template is required. Placeholders: {title} {message}.

say._comment: Escape hatch: run any program. Placeholders: {message} {title} {default} {options}. notify uses the exit code; ask reads stdout; confirm treats exit 0 as approve and nonzero as deny.
